questions.question: Define a blockchain in technical terms.
keyPoints: Data is stored in cryptographically linked blocks
Uses consensus mechanisms to validate transactions

questions.question: Explain the role and structure of a 'block' within a blockchain.
keyPoints: A block is a data structure that batches transactions
It contains a header (with hash, previous block hash, timestamp, nonce) and a list of transactions
The cryptographic hash of the previous block creates the immutable chain

questions.question: Compare and contrast a traditional centralized database with a blockchain, focusing on the technical architecture and its implications for security and trust.
keyPoints: Centralized databases have a single point of control and failure, while blockchains are distributed across a peer-to-peer network

Blockchains use cryptographic hashing and consensus algorithms (e.g., Proof-of-Work) to achieve immutability and trust without a central authority, whereas centralized databases rely on administrator permissions

This distributed architecture makes blockchains more resilient to censorship and single-point attacks but often results in lower transaction throughput and higher latency compared to centralized systems
